title: URL Asset
description: URL reference asset. `url_type` declares the mechanism a receiver uses to invoke the URL (clickthrough vs. tracker_pixel vs. tracker_script) and is distinct from the URL's purpose, which the format declares in `url-asset-requirements.role` (impression_tracker, click_tracker, viewability_tracker, third_party_tracker, clickthrough, landing_page). Senders SHOULD include `url_type` on every URL asset. When `url_type` is absent, receivers SHOULD fall back to the format's `url-asset-requirements.role` (treating clickthrough/landing_page roles as `clickthrough` mechanism and *_tracker roles as `tracker_pixel`); when neither is available, receivers MAY reject the manifest rather than guess.

asset_type.description: Discriminator identifying this as a URL asset. See /schemas/creative/asset-types for the registry.

url.description: URL reference. May be a plain URI or an RFC 6570 URI template carrying AdCP universal macros (e.g., `{SKU}`, `{MEDIA_BUY_ID}`). Buyers MUST NOT pre-encode macro braces at sync time; the ad server URL-encodes substituted values at impression time. See docs/creative/universal-macros.mdx.

url_type.description: Mechanism a receiver uses to invoke this URL (distinct from purpose, which lives in `url-asset-requirements.role`): `clickthrough` for user click destination (landing page), `tracker_pixel` for impression/event tracking via HTTP request (fires GET, expects pixel/204 response), `tracker_script` for measurement SDKs that must load as a <script> tag (OMID verification, native event trackers using method:2). SHOULD be present on every URL asset; senders that omit it force the receiver into the role-based fallback described in this schema's top-level description.

description.description: Description of what this URL points to

provenance.description: Provenance metadata for this asset, overrides manifest-level provenance
